title: Weather news: IMD issues major alert for Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and other states; heavy rain, storms to hit over next 3 days - news24online.com
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued fresh weather alerts for several parts of India, warning of heavy rainfall, thunderstorms, strong winds and continuing heatwave conditions over the next three days. As the southwest monsoon advances rapidly, many regions are expected to receive widespread rain, while parts of Uttar Pradesh are likely to remain under intense heatwave conditions. The forecast also includes strong winds in Delhi, heavy rain in Mumbai and Konkan, and flood-like conditions in parts of Northeast India. Authorities have advised residents to stay updated with local weather warnings and take necessary precautions.
Delhi, Mumbai and Other Major Cities to See Changing Weather
According to the IMD, Delhi is likely to witness a mix of cloudy skies, light rain and thunderstorms during the next three days. Gusty winds reaching speeds of up to 60 kmph may accompany evening showers, offering temporary relief from the summer heat. The maximum temperature in the national capital is expected to remain close to 40 degrees Celsius.
Mumbai and the Konkan region of Maharashtra are expected to receive heavy rainfall until July 2. Coastal districts may also experience strong winds of 40 to 60 kmph, prompting authorities to advise fishermen against venturing into the sea due to rough weather and high waves.
Monsoon Advances Rapidly Across India
The southwest monsoon is progressing quickly across the country and is expected to cover the remaining parts of the north Arabian Sea, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh within the next few days. Weather experts also expect the monsoon to move further into parts of Uttar Pradesh and Uttarakhand soon.
The advancing monsoon is likely to bring much-needed rainfall to several agricultural regions, improving conditions for sowing and farming activities. However, meteorologists warn that heavy rainfall in some areas could also increase the risk of waterlogging, flooding and landslides.
Heatwave Continues to Grip Eastern Uttar Pradesh
While many states prepare for rainfall, eastern Uttar Pradesh is expected to continue facing severe heatwave conditions for the next three days. IMD has warned that some districts may experience extremely hot weather with intense dry winds.
Odisha is also likely to witness hot and humid conditions despite the gradual progress of the monsoon. Residents have been advised to avoid outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours, stay hydrated and follow heat safety guidelines until weather conditions improve.
Northeast and Tamil Nadu Face Heavy Rain Alert
The IMD has issued a serious rainfall warning for several northeastern states, including Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim. Extremely heavy rainfall is expected between June 27 and June 29, raising concerns over flash floods, landslides and river overflow in vulnerable areas.
In South India, Tamil Nadu is also likely to receive heavy rainfall across several districts, including Nilgiris, Coimbatore and Chennai. Thunderstorms, lightning and moderate to heavy showers are expected, while coastal districts such as Nagapattinam and Thoothukudi have been warned about rough sea conditions. Local authorities have restricted marine activities and urged fishermen to remain onshore until conditions improve.
The IMD has advised people across affected states to monitor official weather updates, avoid unnecessary travel during severe weather and follow instructions issued by local administrations to ensure safety during the changing weather conditions.
